context: System topology, release APIs, payload schemas, examples, templates, identifiers, and role enums.

artifacts.usage: System structure for the release coordination benchmark.

nodes.desc: Web Console captures release packet edits from release managers.
API Gateway accepts release packet writes and forwards them to the release service.
Release Service stores release packets and current lifecycle state.
Workflow Engine evaluates approval state and staged rollout policy for each release packet.
Event Bus fans release and incident events to downstream consumers.
Notification Service consumes release and incident events before messaging product, engineering, and support leads.
Primary DB stores release packets, approvals, and change history.

edges.desc: Release managers submit or edit release packets.
API Gateway accepts release packet writes and forwards them to the release service.
Release Service requests approval and rollout evaluation.
Release Service persists canonical packet and state history.
Workflow Engine emits approval and rollout outcomes.
Notification Service consumes release and incident events before messaging product, engineering, and support leads.

endpoints.desc: POST /v1/releases creates a release packet and returns the packet id.

errors.recovery: Use the existing packet or a new key.

endpoints.desc: GET /v1/releases/{releaseId} returns the latest release packet, approval state, and rollout lane.
POST /v1/incidents creates a linked incident record when rollout health degrades.

releaseKey.description: Release packet identifier. Release packet schema requires owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, and communicationPlan.

owner.description: Release packet schema requires owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, and communicationPlan.

riskTier.description: Release packet schema requires owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, and communicationPlan.

rollbackPlan.description: Release packet schema requires owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, and communicationPlan.

communicationPlan.description: Release packet schema requires owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, and communicationPlan.

rolloutWindow.description: Release packet schema requires rolloutWindow.start and rolloutWindow.end in RFC3339 format.

lanes.description: Release packet schema restricts rollout lanes to canary, staged, or full.

content.rollbackPlan: Flip checkout flag and drain queued publish jobs.
content.communicationPlan: Post to launch room and support status channel.
notes: Example packet risk tier is medium and starts with canary before staged rollout.
Example packet rollout window starts at 2026-04-20T09:00:00Z and ends at 2026-04-20T10:00:00Z.
Client template sends releaseKey REL-2026-0420-CHECKOUT to POST /v1/releases.
Client template includes owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, communicationPlan, rolloutWindow, and lanes.
Client template sends an Authorization bearer token.
Config template requires RELEASE_EVENT_TOPIC and NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL.
Config template requires ROLLOUT_WINDOW_TIMEZONE set to UTC.
Config template requires PAIR_SYNC_MODE set to agent-primary.
Release keys match REL-YYYY-MMDD-SLUG in uppercase.
Release key sample REL-2026-0420-CHECKOUT must validate cleanly.
Release key regex prevents ambiguous packet identifiers in docs and APIs.

content.desc: release-manager owns packet quality and approval completeness.
engineering-lead owns rollout safety and rollback execution.
support-lead owns customer-impact communication during release and incident flow.
